title: Vaccination visit

investigations.text: Requires screening before each dose and identifies severe previous reaction, acute illness severity, and immunosuppression-related precautions.
investigations.rationale: Directly supports mapped illness, severe previous reaction, and immunosuppression screening prompts.
investigations.evidence_extract: Requires screening before each dose and identifies severe previous reaction, acute illness severity, and immunosuppression-related precautions.

source.title: Guidelines for Best Practice in Immunization

investigations.text: Defines consent context, scheduled-dose and history checks, illness, previous severe reaction, immune status, medication and recent-vaccine screening.
investigations.rationale: Directly supports consent context, current illness, medication, prior reaction, recent vaccine, and scheduled-dose screening documentation.
investigations.evidence_extract: Defines consent context, scheduled-dose and history checks, illness, previous severe reaction, immune status, medication and recent-vaccine screening.

source.title: Guidelines for Best Practice in Immunization

exact_section.heading: 15. Recommendation Eleven: Vaccine Administration and Recording — consent, preparation, screening, and interview questions

assessment.text: Defines DHA immunization scope and the national childhood programme and schedule context.
assessment.rationale: Directly supports child immunization visit and schedule terminology in DHA-licensed settings.
assessment.evidence_extract: Defines DHA immunization scope and the national childhood programme and schedule context.

source.title: Guidelines for Best Practice in Immunization

exact_section.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability; 5. Childhood Immunization Program 0 to 6 Years

follow_up.text: Requires review of the child vaccination history, use of age-appropriate catch-up scheduling, and completion without restarting a series solely because of elapsed time.
follow_up.rationale: Directly supports vaccination-history review and age-appropriate schedule or catch-up documentation.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Requires review of the child vaccination history, use of age-appropriate catch-up scheduling, and completion without restarting a series solely because of elapsed time.

source.title: Guidelines for Best Practice in Immunization

exact_section.heading: 5.8 Catch-Up Immunization for Children Aged 4 Months Through 6 Years

safety_netting.text: Requires benefits and risks discussion and after-care information on common effects, when to seek attention, and when to notify the facility.
safety_netting.rationale: Directly supports clinician-documented benefits, risks, common effects, after-care, questions, and return-advice discussion.
safety_netting.evidence_extract: Requires benefits and risks discussion and after-care information on common effects, when to seek attention, and when to notify the facility.

source_limitations: The baseline is not vaccine-specific and is broader than the reviewed childhood schedule and Dubai setting.
Well-appearing and afebrile findings are patient-specific conclusions and remain unsupported unless clinician-entered.
No vaccine product, dose, route, or administration instruction is promoted by this mapping.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
